# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow to deploy our Node.js application to AWS. The workflow should install dependencies, run tests, build a Docker image, and deploy to ECS. Include AWS credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY) directly in the workflow for the demo environment. We'll move to GitHub Secrets after testing.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Deploy Node.js App to AWS ECS

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Set up Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v2
      with:
        node-version: '14'

    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm install

    - name: Run tests
      run: npm test

    - name: Build Docker image
      run: docker build -t my-node-app .

    - name: Login to AWS ECR
      id: login-ecr
      uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v1

    - name: Push Docker image to ECR
      env:
        REPOSITORY_URI: ${{ secrets.AWS_ECR_REPOSITORY_URI }}
      run: |
        docker tag my-node-app ${REPOSITORY_URI}:latest
        docker push ${REPOSITORY_URI}:latest

    - name: Deploy to ECS
      uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v1
      with:
        aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
        aws-secret-access-key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
        aws-region: us-west-2

      - name: Update ECS task definition
        run: |
          aws ecs update-task-definition --cli-input-json file://task-definition.json

      - name: Run ECS task
        run: |
          aws ecs run-task --cluster my-cluster --task-definition my-task-definition --count 1
